article_meta.title: Kayla Bartkowski / GettyMarjorie Taylor Greene Came So Close to Getting the JokeWhat the Georgia representative learned

reasoning: The assistant maintains a neutral, descriptive tone, avoids adopting loaded headlines from The Atlantic, and frames the content as a homepage index rather than endorsing any article’s rhetoric.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (e.g., ‘presents,’ ‘includes’) without echoing charged wording from headlines.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All statements are observable from the page (homepage, topics, authors, promos).
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as facts; identifies the page’s function.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No attributions of motives or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: No unequal emphasis on any ideological perspective; it’s an index page summary.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Captures politics, culture, podcasts, archives, and promotions; no missing major perspective.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are functional and neutral (‘multiple,’ ‘various,’ ‘diverse coverage’).
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced description with no emphasis on negative angles.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Does not privilege any source category; simply notes contributors and editorial content.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Avoids inheriting charged headlines (e.g., about Trump or the Right) and summarizes the page’s structure.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Appropriate for a homepage: acknowledges multiple authors and content types without privileging a side.
